channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump vows to 'take care of Cuba,' praises Venezuela cooperation at summit
item.description: Trump made the promise in front of an assembled meeting of Latin American leaders.
item.content:encoded:
Trump made the promise in front of an assembled meeting of Latin American leaders.

(Image credit: Rebecca Blackwell)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: The impact of Iran's attacks on the UAE
item.description: The UAE, billed as a global luxury destination, has received the bulk of Iran's attacks in the region so far.
item.content:encoded: The UAE, billed as a global luxury destination, has received the bulk of Iran's attacks in the region so far.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: Middle East war enters its 8th day
item.description: The U.S. and Israeli war with Iran has entered a second week, with drones and missiles crisscrossing much of the Middle East and death tolls rising.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. and Israeli war with Iran has entered a second week, with drones and missiles crisscrossing much of the Middle East and death tolls rising.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Kahn

item.title: British Columbia moves to permanent daylight saving time
item.description: The premier of the Canadian province of British Columbia, David Eby, explains why this Sunday is the beginning of a new era of permanent daylight saving time there.
item.content:encoded: The premier of the Canadian province of British Columbia, David Eby, explains why this Sunday is the beginning of a new era of permanent daylight saving time there.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: How the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran is realigning the politics of the Middle East
item.description: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with author and journalist Kim Ghattas about the impacts the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran will have on the broader region.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with author and journalist Kim Ghattas about the impacts the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran will have on the broader region.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levitt

item.title: Daughters of Donbas band sings songs of stolen Ukrainian children
item.description: A group of Ukrainian musicians is touring the world to remind people that thousands of Ukrainian children have been kidnapped by Russia.
item.content:encoded: A group of Ukrainian musicians is touring the world to remind people that thousands of Ukrainian children have been kidnapped by Russia.
item.dc:creator: Robbie Griffiths

item.title: European leaders' views on the developing war in the Middle East
item.description: Europeans are divided over how to respond to the US/Israel war with Iran. German leaders have been measured, while Spain's prime minister has been critical of the Trump Administration and of Israel.
item.content:encoded: Europeans are divided over how to respond to the US/Israel war with Iran. German leaders have been measured, while Spain's prime minister has been critical of the Trump Administration and of Israel.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: At the border between Iran and Turkey, some flee the war, while others return
item.description: Iranians are streaming across the border with Turkey, fleeing constant bombardment. But some are also going the other way -- returning to Iran out of worry for loved ones they can't otherwise reach.
item.content:encoded: Iranians are streaming across the border with Turkey, fleeing constant bombardment. But some are also going the other way -- returning to Iran out of worry for loved ones they can't otherwise reach.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: An international security expert analyzes regional impact of U.S.-Israel war on Iran
item.description: NPR's Scott Simon talks to Michael Wahid Hanna of the International Crisis Group about how the spillover of the war between Iran, Israel, and the U.S. into other countries might change regional dynamics.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Simon talks to Michael Wahid Hanna of the International Crisis Group about how the spillover of the war between Iran, Israel, and the U.S. into other countries might change regional dynamics.
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: Trump warns Iran 'will be hit very hard' as war enters its second week
item.description: Trump warned Saturday that Iran would soon "be hit very hard," adding that the U.S. may expand its target list to "new areas and groups of people."
item.content:encoded: Trump warned Saturday that Iran would soon "be hit very hard," adding that the U.S. may expand its target list to "new areas and groups of people."
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim
